title: Fake Recruiter — Data & Payment Trap
intro: A 'recruiter' offers a job quickly. Your goal: verify legitimacy, avoid giving sensitive info, and never pay for 'processing'.
advice: Verify recruiters via official company channels and real job postings.
Never pay fees to get hired.
Do not send SSN, bank details, or ID scans to unverified contacts.

r1.text: Hi! I’m Mia from Hiring. Your resume was shortlisted. We can offer you $32/hr. Can you start this week?

choices.label: What company is this and can you share the official job posting link (not a shortened link)?
choices.explain: Good: you’re asking for verifiable details.

choices.label: Yes, I can start. What do you need from me?
choices.explain: Risky: moving too fast is a common scam pattern.

choices.label: Sounds great. Send me the offer letter.
choices.explain: Neutral: paperwork is normal, but still verify the sender.

r2.text: We’re moving fast. To proceed, send your full name, address, and a photo of your ID so we can 'activate' payroll.

choices.label: No. I will only provide sensitive info through an official portal after verifying the company.
choices.explain: Correct: sensitive info should go through trusted systems.

choices.label: Okay, here is my ID photo and address.
I can share my name, but not my ID. What’s your company email domain?
choices.explain: Better than sending ID, but scammers can spoof domains too.

r3.text: Great. We need a background check. Pay a $49 processing fee today and we’ll reimburse you with your first paycheck.

choices.label: No legitimate employer asks candidates to pay fees. I’m ending this conversation.
choices.explain: Correct: job fee scams are common.

choices.label: Okay, where do I pay?
choices.explain: Compromised: you’re about to lose money and expose payment details.

choices.label: Can you send an invoice?
choices.explain: Still bad: the fee itself is the red flag.

r4.text: We don’t have time. If you don’t comply in 10 minutes we’ll move on to the next candidate.

choices.label: That’s fine. I’ll apply through the official website only. Goodbye.
choices.explain: Correct: pressure tactics are a scam hallmark.

choices.label: Wait—okay, I’ll send what you asked for.

end_good.text: ✅ Safe outcome: You refused fees and protected sensitive information by verifying through official channels.

end_bad.text: ❌ Compromised: You shared sensitive identity info with an unverified contact.

end_bad_fee.text: ❌ Compromised: You paid a scam fee / exposed payment details. Legit employers do not require upfront candidate fees.
